arts.ttl: Serie A | Juventus v Napoli: Probable starting lineups
arts.sum: Scottish international Scott McTominay is set to make his starting debut for Napoli against Juventus, while Bianconeri youngster Nicolò Savona may get the nod for Federico Gatti. Gatti, who suffered an ankle injury during Juventus’ 3-1 victory over PSV Eindhoven in the Champions League, has reportedly recovered. Coach Thiago Motta confirmed in his pre-match press conference that Gatti will be “available,” but according to Sky Sport Italia, he is not yet fully fit. This situation could lead Motta to shift Pierre Kalulu to center-back and start Savona as a right-back. arts.ttl: Cannavaro: ‘Juve was an education for me, I would have stayed at Napoli forever’
arts.sum: Former Napoli and Juventus defender Fabio Cannavaro previewed the upcoming match between the two sides, explaining who can edge the match. Speaking with La Gazzetta dello Sport, Cannavaro talked about his time in Naples and Turin, sharing experiences of playing for both sides, and why Juve was ‘ a school’ for him where he learned so much: Do you remember what you were doing on the afternoon of March 7, 1993? “Do you?” You were a young man of 19 years… “Who was making his Serie A debut, in Turin, in a Juventus-Napoli that ended 4-3. arts.ttl: Juventus vs. Napoli: first big match of the day
arts.sum: Juventus have won 10 of their last 13 home games against Napoli in Serie A, including last season (1-0); in this period there has been one draw and two wins for the Azzurri, both 1-0 in 2017-18 and 2022-23. Napoli have won six of their last nine games against Juventus in Serie A (D1 L2), as many wins as in the previous 22 meetings between these two teams in the competition (D4 L12).
